Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider points $$P ( -1 , 3 ) ,$$ $$Q ( 1 , 5 ) ,$$ and $$R ( -3 , 7 ) .$$ Determine the requested vectors and express each of them a. in component form and b. by using the standard unit vectors.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider points $$P ( -1 , 3 ) ,$$ $$Q ( 1 , 5 ) ,$$ and $$R ( -3 , 7 ) .$$ Determine the requested vectors and express each of them a. in component form and b. by using the standard unit vectors.
Question: $$\vec{P Q} + \vec{P R}$$
Answer: a. $$\vec{P Q} + \vec{P R} = \langle 0 , 6 \rangle ;$$ b. $$\overset{\rightarrow}{P Q} + \overset{\rightarrow}{P R} = 6 \textbf{j}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider points $$P ( -1 , 3 ) ,$$ $$Q ( 1 , 5 ) ,$$ and $$R ( -3 , 7 ) .$$ Determine the requested vectors and express each of them a. in component form and b. by using the standard unit vectors.
Question: $$\vec{P Q} - \vec{P R}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider points $$P ( -1 , 3 ) ,$$ $$Q ( 1 , 5 ) ,$$ and $$R ( -3 , 7 ) .$$ Determine the requested vectors and express each of them a. in component form and b. by using the standard unit vectors.
Question: $$2 \vec{P Q} - 2 \vec{P R}$$
Answer: a. $$2 \vec{P Q} - 2 \vec{P R} = \langle 8 , -4 \rangle ;$$ b. $$2 \overset{\rightarrow}{P Q} - 2 \overset{\rightarrow}{P R} = 8 \textbf{i} - 4 \textbf{j}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider points $$P ( -1 , 3 ) ,$$ $$Q ( 1 , 5 ) ,$$ and $$R ( -3 , 7 ) .$$ Determine the requested vectors and express each of them a. in component form and b. by using the standard unit vectors.
Question: A vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ has initial point $$( -1 , -3 )$$ and terminal point $$( 2 , 1 ) .$$ Find the unit vector in the direction of $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express the answer in component form.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider points $$P ( -1 , 3 ) ,$$ $$Q ( 1 , 5 ) ,$$ and $$R ( -3 , 7 ) .$$ Determine the requested vectors and express each of them a. in component form and b. by using the standard unit vectors.
Question: The vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ has initial point $$P ( 1 , 0 )$$ and terminal point $$Q$$ that is on the y -axis and above the initial point. Find the coordinates of terminal point $$Q$$ such that the magnitude of the vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is $$\sqrt{5} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider points $$P ( -1 , 3 ) ,$$ $$Q ( 1 , 5 ) ,$$ and $$R ( -3 , 7 ) .$$ Determine the requested vectors and express each of them a. in component form and b. by using the standard unit vectors.
Question: The vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ has initial point $$P ( 1 , 1 )$$ and terminal point $$Q$$ that is on the x -axis and left of the initial point. Find the coordinates of terminal point $$Q$$ such that the magnitude of the vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is $$\sqrt{10} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: $$\textbf{a} = 2 \textbf{i} + \textbf{j} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} = \textbf{i} + 3 \textbf{j}$$
Answer: a. $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} = 3 \textbf{i} + 4 \textbf{j} ,$$ $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} = \langle 3 , 4 \rangle ;$$ b. $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b} = \textbf{i} - 2 \textbf{j} ,$$ $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b} = \langle 1 , -2 \rangle ;$$ c. Answers will vary; d. $$2 \textbf{a} = 4 \textbf{i} + 2 \textbf{j} ,$$ $$2 \textbf{a} = \langle 4 , 2 \rangle ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} = - \textbf{i} - 3 \textbf{j} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} = \langle -1 , -3 \rangle ,$$ $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} = 3 \textbf{i} - \textbf{j} ,$$ $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} = \langle 3 , -1 \rangle$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: $$\textbf{a} = 2 \textbf{i} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} = -2 \textbf{i} + 2 \textbf{j}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: Let $$\textbf{a}$$ be a standard-position vector with terminal point $$( -2 , -4 ) .$$ Let $$\textbf{b}$$ be a vector with initial point $$( 1 , 2 )$$ and terminal point $$( -1 , 4 ) .$$ Find the magnitude of vector $$-3 \textbf{a} + \textbf{b} - 4 \textbf{i} + \textbf{j} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: Let $$\textbf{a}$$ be a standard-position vector with terminal point at $$( 2 , 5 ) .$$ Let $$\textbf{b}$$ be a vector with initial point $$( -1 , 3 )$$ and terminal point $$( 1 , 0 ) .$$ Find the magnitude of vector $$\textbf{a} - 3 \textbf{b} + 14 \textbf{i} - 14 \textbf{j} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: Let $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v}$$ be two nonzero vectors that are nonequivalent. Consider the vectors $$\textbf{a} = 4 \textbf{u} + 5 \textbf{v}$$ and $$\textbf{b} = \textbf{u} + 2 \textbf{v}$$ defined in terms of $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v} .$$ Find the scalar $$\lambda$$ such that vectors $$\textbf{a} + \lambda \textbf{b}$$ and $$\textbf{u} - \textbf{v}$$ are equivalent.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: Let $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v}$$ be two nonzero vectors that are nonequivalent. Consider the vectors $$\textbf{a} = 2 \textbf{u} - 4 \textbf{v}$$ and $$\textbf{b} = 3 \textbf{u} - 7 \textbf{v}$$ defined in terms of $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v} .$$ Find the scalars $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$ such that vectors $$\alpha \textbf{a} + \beta \textbf{b}$$ and $$\textbf{u} - \textbf{v}$$ are equivalent.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: Consider the vector $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. t \left.\right) = \langle \text{cos} t , \text{sin} t \rangle$$ with components that depend on a real number $$t .$$ As the number $$t$$ varies, the components of $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. t \left.\right)$$ change as well, depending on the functions that define them. Write the vectors $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. 0 \left.\right)$$ and $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. \pi \left.\right)$$ in component form. Show that the magnitude $$\parallel \textbf{a} \left(\right. t \left.\right) \parallel$$ of vector $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. t \left.\right)$$ remains constant for any real number $$t .$$ As $$t$$ varies, show that the terminal point of vector $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. t \left.\right)$$ describes a circle centered at the origin of radius $$1 .$$
Answer: a. $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. 0 \left.\right) = \langle 1 , 0 \rangle ,$$ $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. \pi \left.\right) = \langle -1 , 0 \rangle ;$$ b. Answers may vary; c. Answers may vary

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: Consider vector $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \langle x , \sqrt{1 - x^{2}} \rangle$$ with components that depend on a real number $$x \in [ -1 , 1 ] .$$ As the number $$x$$ varies , the components of $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ change as well, depending on the functions that define them. Write the vectors $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. 0 \left.\right)$$ and $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. 1 \left.\right)$$ in component form. Show that the magnitude $$\parallel \textbf{a} \left(\right. x \left.\right) \parallel$$ of vector $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ remains constant for any real number $$x$$ As $$x$$ varies, show that if $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ is in standard position, then its terminal point describes a semicircle. Why is it only a semicircle?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: Show that vectors $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. t \left.\right) = \langle \text{cos} t , \text{sin} t \rangle$$ and $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \langle x , \sqrt{1 - x^{2}} \rangle$$ are equivalent for $$x = 1$$ and $$t = 2 k \pi ,$$ where $$k$$ is an integer.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use the given vectors $$\textbf{a}$$ and $$\textbf{b} .$$ a) Determine the vector sum $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. b) Find the vector difference $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ and express it in both the component form and by using the standard unit vectors. c) Verify that the vectors $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} + \textbf{b} ,$$ and, respectively, $$\textbf{a} ,$$ $$\textbf{b} ,$$ and $$\textbf{a} - \textbf{b}$$ satisfy the triangle inequality. d) Determine the vectors $$2 \textbf{a} ,$$ $$- \textbf{b} ,$$ and $$2 \textbf{a} - \textbf{b} .$$ Express the vectors in both the component form and by using standard unit vectors.
Question: Show that vectors $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. t \left.\right) = \langle \text{cos} t , \text{sin} t \rangle$$ and $$\textbf{a} \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \langle x , \sqrt{1 - x^{2}} \rangle$$ are opposite for $$x = 1$$ and $$t = \pi + 2 k \pi ,$$ where $$k$$ is an integer.

Question: $$\parallel \textbf{u} \parallel = 8 ,$$ $$\theta = \pi$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the component form of vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} ,$$ given its magnitude and the angle the vector makes with the positive x -axis. Give exact answers when possible.
Question: $$\Vert \mathbf{\text{u}} \Vert = 10 ,$$ $$\theta = \frac{5 \pi}{6}$$

Question: $$\Vert \mathbf{\text{u}} \Vert = 50 ,$$ $$\theta = \frac{3 \pi}{4}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: $$\textbf{u} = 5 \sqrt{2} \textbf{i} - 5 \sqrt{2} \textbf{j}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: $$\textbf{u} = - \sqrt{3} \textbf{i} - \textbf{j}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: Let $$\textbf{a} = \langle a_{1} , a_{2} \rangle ,$$ $$\textbf{b} = \langle b_{1} , b_{2} \rangle ,$$ and $$\textbf{c} = \langle c_{1} , c_{2} \rangle$$ be three nonzero vectors. If $$a_{1} b_{2} - a_{2} b_{1} \ne 0 ,$$ then show there are two scalars, $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta ,$$ such that $$\textbf{c} = \alpha \textbf{a} + \beta \textbf{b} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: Consider vectors $$\textbf{a} = \langle 2 , -4 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{b}} = \langle -1 , 2 \rangle ,$$ and c = 0 Determine the scalars $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$ such that $$\textbf{c} = \alpha \textbf{a} + \beta \textbf{b} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: Let $$P ( x_{0} , f ( x_{0} ) )$$ be a fixed point on the graph of the differentiable function $$f$$ with a domain that is the set of real numbers. Determine the real number $$z_{0}$$ such that point $$Q ( x_{0} + 1 , z_{0} )$$ is situated on the line tangent to the graph of $$f$$ at point $$P .$$ Determine the unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ with initial point $$P$$ in the direction of vector $$P Q .$$
Answer: a. $$z_{0} = f ( x_{0} ) + f^{\prime} ( x_{0} ) ;$$ b. $$\textbf{u} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 + \left(\left[\right. f^{'} \left(\right. x_{0} \left.\right) \left]\right.\right)^{2}}} \langle 1 , f^{'} \left(\right. x_{0} \left.\right) \rangle$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: Consider the function $$f ( x ) = x^{4} ,$$ where $$x \in \RR .$$ Determine the real number $$z_{0}$$ such that point $$Q ( 2 , z_{0} )$$ s situated on the line tangent to the graph of $$f$$ at point $$P ( 1 , 1 ) .$$ Determine the unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ with initial point $$P$$ and terminal point $$Q .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: Consider $$f$$ and $$g$$ two functions defined on the same set of real numbers $$D .$$ Let $$\textbf{a} = \langle x , f \left(\right. x \left.\right) \rangle$$ and $$\textbf{b} = \langle x , g \left(\right. x \left.\right) \rangle$$ be two vectors that describe the graphs of the functions, where $$x \in D .$$ Show that if the graphs of the functions $$f$$ and $$g$$ do not intersect, then the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{a}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{b}}$$ are not equivalent.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: Find $$x \in \RR$$ such that vectors $$\textbf{a} = \langle x , \text{sin} x \rangle$$ and $$\textbf{b} = \langle x , \text{cos} x \rangle$$ are equivalent.

Question: Calculate the coordinates of point $$D$$ such that $$A B C D$$ is a parallelogram, with $$A ( 1 , 1 ) ,$$ $$B ( 2 , 4 ) ,$$ and $$C ( 7 , 4 ) .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: Consider the points $$A ( 2 , 1 ) ,$$ $$B ( 10 , 6 ) ,$$ $$C ( 13 , 4 ) ,$$ and $$D ( 16 , -2 ) .$$ Determine the component form of vector $$\vec{A D} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: The speed of an object is the magnitude of its related velocity vector. A football thrown by a quarterback has an initial speed of $$70$$ mph and an angle of elevation of $$30 \text{°} .$$ Determine the velocity vector in mph and express it in component form. (Round to two decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: A baseball player throws a baseball at an angle of $$30 \text{°}$$ with the horizontal. If the initial speed of the ball is $$100$$ mph, find the horizontal and vertical components of the initial velocity vector of the baseball. (Round to two decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: A bullet is fired with an initial velocity of $$1500$$ ft/sec at an angle of $$60 \text{°}$$ with the horizontal. Find the horizontal and vertical components of the velocity vector of the bullet. (Round to two decimal places.)
Answer: The horizontal and vertical components are $$750$$ ft/sec and $$1299.04$$ ft/sec, respectively.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] A 65-kg sprinter exerts a force of $$798$$ N at a $$19 \text{°}$$ angle with respect to the ground on the starting block at the instant a race begins. Find the horizontal component of the force. (Round to two decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] Two forces, a horizontal force of $$45$$ lb and another of $$52$$ lb, act on the same object. The angle between these forces is $$25 \text{°} .$$ Find the magnitude and direction angle from the positive x -axis of the resultant force that acts on the object. (Round to two decimal places.)
Answer: The magnitude of resultant force is $$94.71$$ lb; the direction angle is $$13.42 \text{°} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] Two forces, a vertical force of $$26$$ lb and another of $$45$$ lb, act on the same object. The angle between these forces is $$55 \text{°} .$$ Find the magnitude and direction angle from the positive x -axis of the resultant force that acts on the object. (Round to two decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] Three forces act on object. Two of the forces have the magnitudes $$58$$ N and $$27$$ N, and make angles $$53 \text{°}$$ and $$152 \text{°} ,$$ respectively, with the positive x -axis. Find the magnitude and the direction angle from the positive x -axis of the third force such that the resultant force acting on the object is zero. (Round to two decimal places.)
Answer: The magnitude of the third vector is $$60.03$$ N; the direction angle is $$259.38 \text{°} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: Three forces with magnitudes $$80$$ lb, $$120$$ lb, and $$60$$ lb act on an object at angles of $$45 \text{°} ,$$ $$60 \text{°}$$ and $$30 \text{°} ,$$ respectively, with the positive x -axis. Find the magnitude and direction angle from the positive x -axis of the resultant force. (Round to two decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] An airplane is flying in the direction of $$43 \text{°}$$ east of north (also abbreviated as $$\text{N} 43 \text{E} )$$ at a speed of $$550$$ mph. A wind with speed $$25$$ mph comes from the southwest at a bearing of $$\text{N} 15 \text{E} .$$ What are the ground speed and new direction of the airplane?
Answer: The new ground speed of the airplane is $$572.19$$ mph; the new direction is $$\text{N} 41.82 \text{E} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] A boat is traveling in the water at $$30$$ mph in a direction of $$\text{N} 20 \text{E}$$ (that is, $$20 \text{°}$$ east of north). A strong current is moving at $$15$$ mph in a direction of $$\text{N} 45 \text{E} .$$ What are the new speed and direction of the boat?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] A 50-lb weight is hung by a cable so that the two portions of the cable make angles of $$40 \text{°}$$ and $$53 \text{°} ,$$ respectively, with the horizontal. Find the magnitudes of the forces of tension $$\text{T}_{1}$$ and $$\text{T}_{2}$$ in the cables if the resultant force acting on the object is zero. (Round to two decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] A 62-lb weight hangs from a rope that makes the angles of $$29 \text{°}$$ and $$61 \text{°} ,$$ respectively, with the horizontal. Find the magnitudes of the forces of tension $$\text{T}_{1}$$ and $$\text{T}_{2}$$ in the cables if the resultant force acting on the object is zero. (Round to two decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] A 1500-lb boat is parked on a ramp that makes an angle of $$30 \text{°}$$ with the horizontal. The boat’s weight vector points downward and is a sum of two vectors: a horizontal vector $$\left(\textbf{v}\right)_{1}$$ that is parallel to the ramp and a vertical vector $$\left(\textbf{v}\right)_{2}$$ that is perpendicular to the inclined surface. The magnitudes of vectors $$\left(\textbf{v}\right)_{1}$$ and $$\left(\textbf{v}\right)_{2}$$ are the horizontal and vertical component, respectively, of the boat’s weight vector. Find the magnitudes of $$\left(\textbf{v}\right)_{1}$$ and $$\left(\textbf{v}\right)_{2} .$$ (Round to the nearest integer.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: [T] An 85-lb box is at rest on a $$26 \text{°}$$ incline. Determine the magnitude of the force parallel to the incline necessary to keep the box from sliding. (Round to the nearest integer.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: A guy-wire supports a pole that is $$75$$ ft high. One end of the wire is attached to the top of the pole and the other end is anchored to the ground $$50$$ ft from the base of the pole. Determine the horizontal and vertical components of the force of tension in the wire if its magnitude is $$50$$ lb. (Round to the nearest integer.)
Answer: The two horizontal and vertical components of the force of tension are $$28$$ lb and $$42$$ lb, respectively.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, vector $$\textbf{u}$$ is given. Find the angle $$\theta \in [ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ that vector $$\textbf{u}$$ makes with the positive direction of the x -axis, in a counter-clockwise direction.
Question: A telephone pole guy-wire has an angle of elevation of $$35 \text{°}$$ with respect to the ground. The force of tension in the guy-wire is $$120$$ lb. Find the horizontal and vertical components of the force of tension. (Round to the nearest integer.)
